title: How to Get Rid of Dry Cough: 8 Powerful Herbal Decoctions to Use

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and coughing.
Flavonoids are good for dry cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.

Triterpenoids in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x7c6439488cf0> Sambucus nigra have been shown to be good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing coughing and making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is a popular herb used for centuries to soothe dry cough. The root of this plant contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When made into a decoction, the licorice root helps to calm the irritated throat tissues and reduce inflammation. This leads to a decrease in coughing and discomfort. The decoction also helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, which is beneficial for dry cough caused by postnasal drip or sinusitis. Additionally, licorice root has antibacterial properties that can help combat infections that may be contributing to the dry cough. When consumed as a decoction, the active compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra are absorbed into the bloodstream, providing quick relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra herbal decoction to help with a dry cough, start by gathering dried elderberry flowers, roots, or berries. You can buy these at a local health food store or online. Begin by measuring out 1-2 teaspoons of the dried Sambucus nigra per cup of water. Next, boil fresh, filtered water in a pot and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add the dried Sambucus nigra to the water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, to make the decoction more palatable. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herbs, and discard the solids. Finally, drink the decoction hot or warm, up to 3-4 times a day, to help soothe and relieve your dry cough symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for dry cough because it has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from the congestion and discomfort of dry coughing.
Carvacrol is good for dry cough because it has natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to cough and recover.
Roscmarinic acid is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, reducing inflammation and relieving dry cough symptoms.
Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris decoctions, also known as thyme, is a natural remedy for dry cough. The leaves of the thyme plant contain a compound called thymol, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink thyme decoction, the thymol helps to soothe the throat and calm the cough. This is especially helpful for people with dry cough, as it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme decoction has a warm and comforting effect on the body, which can help to relax the muscles in the throat and reduce coughing. Thyme is also rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, and support overall health. Thyme decoctions are easy to make and can be drunk as a tea, making it a convenient and accessible remedy for dry cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal decoctions that help with dry cough, start by gathering fresh or dried elderflower (Sambucus nigra) leaves and flowers. You can use either fresh or dried herbs, but dried ones are more convenient. If using fresh herbs, dry them in a low-temperature oven or with a food dehydrator for 2-3 hours. Next, combine 2-3 tablespoons of dried or 4-6 tablespoons of fresh elderflower with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce heat to a gentle simmer, then let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl or container, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool, then store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 days. To use, take 1/4 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help soothe a dry cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the airways and relieve irritation, allowing for easier mucus production and cough relief.
Flavonoids are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity and duration of dry cough symptoms.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.

Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect the throat from irritation and infection, thus easing the symptoms of a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions can be beneficial for a dry cough due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. When we have a dry cough, our throat and lungs are irritated, causing pain and discomfort. The decoction of Echinacea purpurea, a type of flowering plant, contains compounds like alkylamides and glycosides that help reduce inflammation and soothe the irritated tissues. This can bring quick relief from the pain and discomfort associated with a dry cough. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cough. This can help the body recover faster and reduce the severity of the symptoms. The decoction can be consumed as a tea or added to warm water to help ease the dry cough and provide relief from the associated discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal decoctions for a dry cough, start by gathering the dried flowers and leaves of the elder tree, also known as Sambucus nigra. Use about one teaspoon of the dried herb for every cup of water you plan to use. If you're making a decoction for a child, use half the amount of herb. Boil the water in a pot, then add the dried Sambucus nigra herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the decoction will be. After the steeping time is over, remove the herb from the pot and let the decoction cool down. Once it's cool enough, you can drink it as is or add honey to sweeten it. Drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help soothe and relieve your dry cough. You can also add it to warm tea or milk to make it more comforting.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for dry cough because it helps thin and break down mucus, making it easier to cough up, and also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and airways.
Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus.
Eucalyptol is good for dry cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving the cough.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they have antitussive properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion, and providing quick relief from dry cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions have been used for centuries to help relieve a dry cough. The leaves of the Eucalyptus tree contain a special oil that acts as an expectorant, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways. When you drink Eucalyptus globulus decoctions, the oil is released into your body and reaches your lungs, where it helps break down the thick mucus that's causing your cough. This makes it easier to cough up the mucus and brings relief from the discomfort. The decoction also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the throat and reduce swelling. As a result, your dry cough becomes less severe and you can breathe more easily.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra herbal decoction that helps with a dry cough, start by gathering the right materials. Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberry, is the key herb in this remedy. You'll need a handful of dried Sambucus nigra flowers, a large pot, and a measuring cup. Begin by boiling four cups of water in the pot. Next, add a generous handful of the dried flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herb's properties to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture into a separate container, discarding the solids, and then add a sweetener like honey if desired. The resulting decoction can be taken three times a day to help soothe a dry cough. It's essential to note that this remedy should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and consult a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for dry cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to cough up mucus and recover from the infection.

Shogaols are good for dry cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they help reduce inflammation in the throat and act as a natural expectorant, loosening and clearing mucus to ease coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions, also known as ginger, is a traditional remedy for dry cough. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. When you have a dry cough, the airways in your lungs are inflamed, making it hard to cough up mucus. Ginger decoction helps to reduce this inflammation, making it easier to breathe. The expectorant properties in ginger also help to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, relieving the cough. Additionally, ginger has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat the underlying infection causing the dry cough. Drinking ginger decoction can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms, especially when consumed warm. The warm liquid helps to soothe the throat and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal decoctions that help with dry cough, you will need some fresh or dried elderflower. Start by gathering 2-3 tablespoons of the flowers, or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers. Next, boil 1 liter of water and then add the elderflowers, making sure they are completely submerged. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes. Remove the flowers by straining the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Discard the solids and let the decoction cool down. Once it's at room temperature, you can store it in the fridge for up to 3 days. To use, take 1-2 tablespoons of the decoction, 2-3 times a day, when needed, and mix it with a small amount of honey to make it more palatable. However, please consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using Sambucus nigra herbal decoctions, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Roscmarinic acid is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the irritated throat, reducing the urge to cough and promoting a faster recovery.
Menthol is good for dry cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and relieving the discomfort of a dry, scratchy throat.
Quercetin is good for dry cough because it helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, allowing mucus to break down and be coughed up more easily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita decoctions, also known as peppermint, can help with dry cough. The menthol in peppermint works by thinning and loosening mucus in the throat, making it easier to cough up. When you have a dry cough, the mucus is thick and sticky, making it hard to cough up. The menthol in peppermint helps to break down this mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving the cough. Additionally, peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the throat and reduce irritation. This can help to calm the cough and provide relief. Peppermint's cooling sensation can also help to numb the throat, making it feel more comfortable when you cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra herbal decoction to help with dry cough, start by gathering dried Sambucus nigra flowers, also known as elderflower. Use one teaspoon of dried flowers for every 250ml of water. Place the flowers in a saucepan and add the water, then bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a slice of fresh ginger to enhance the flavor and help reduce the cough. After the decoction has cooled, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids and drink the decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals, to help soothe your dry cough. You can also add a tablespoon of honey to sweeten the flavor and make it more palatable. It's essential to note that while Sambucus nigra decoctions can help alleviate dry cough symptoms, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for dry cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, providing quick relief from dry cough and soreness.
Flavonoids are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to cough up mucus and feel relieved.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they help to reduce inflammation in the throat and relieve irritation, making it easier to swallow and breathe.
Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus decoctions, also known as mullein, are a natural remedy for dry cough. The leaves and flowers of the plant contain powerful anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe the respiratory tract. When made into a decoction, these properties are released, providing quick relief from dry, hacking coughs. The anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the throat and airways, while the antispasmodic properties calm the muscles that cause coughing. This helps to break the coughing cycle, allowing the body to heal and recover faster. Mullein decoctions are often used as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. Regular use of mullein decoctions can also help to strengthen the immune system and reduce the frequency and severity of dry coughs.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra herbal decoction that helps with dry cough, start by gathering fresh or dried elderberry flowers and berries. If using fresh, gently wash them in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Combine 2-3 tablespoons of the flowers and berries with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool, then refrigerate it for later use. To take the decoction, drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed. It's essential to note that Sambucus nigra can interact with certain medications and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe the throat, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieving the discomfort of a dry cough.

Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the dry, scratchy sensation caused by a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra decoctions, also known as elderberry, can help with a dry cough. The reason is that it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the dryness and irritation that causes a cough. The decoction also contains sambunigrin, a compound that can loosen mucus and help bring it up. When mucus is loosened and removed, the cough can become less severe. Additionally, Sambucus nigra decoctions have been traditionally used to treat colds and flu, which often cause dry coughs. The decoction's antiviral properties can help fight off the underlying infection, allowing the body to recover and the cough to subside.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal decoctions that help with dry cough, start by gathering dried elderberry flowers, leaves, and berries. Use one tablespoon of the dried mixture for every 250ml of boiling water. Place the dried mixture in a saucepan and add the boiling water, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Next, strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids, and then let it cool. You can drink the decoction as is, or add a spoonful of honey to make it sweeter and easier to swallow. It's recommended to drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help relieve dry cough, but always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.

intro_desc: A dry cough can be a real nuisance, making it hard to sleep and even harder to breathe. When you have a dry cough, your airways are inflamed and irritated, leading to a constant, hacking cough. To soothe a dry cough, you can make a herbal decoction, a mixture of herbs that are steeped in hot water to release their healing properties. Two herbs that are particularly effective in calming a dry cough are 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (also known as licorice root) and 'Thymus vulgaris' (also known as thyme). Glycyrrhiza glabra is great at reducing inflammation in the airways and has anti-microbial properties that help fight off infections that can cause a cough. Thymus vulgaris, on the other hand, has a soothing effect on the throat and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm a dry cough.  Fixing the issue of a dry cough is important because it can disrupt your sleep, making you feel tired and irritable during the day. A dry cough can also lead to complications like bronchitis or pneumonia if left untreated. By using a herbal decoction made with Glycyrrhiza glabra and Thymus vulgaris, you can start to feel better and breathe easier. Drinking the decoction regularly can help reduce the frequency and severity of your cough, making it easier to sleep and enjoy your daily activities.
supplementary_best: To remedy dry cough, a combination of herbal decoctions from 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', 'Eucalyptus globulus', and 'Verbascum thapsus' is effective. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce irritation. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus leaf, contains compounds that help break down mucus, making it easier to cough up. Verbascum thapsus, or mullein leaf, has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. This combination provides a triple-action approach to addressing dry cough, reducing inflammation, breaking down mucus, and loosening and clearing it from the body. When consumed as a decoction, these herbs can help provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a dry cough, it's essential to avoid herbal decoctions that can exacerbate the condition. One such herb is Lobelia inflata, also known as Lobelia or Indian tobacco. Lobelia contains a compound called lobeline, which can dry out the airways and worsen a dry cough. Another herb to avoid is Ephedra sinica, also known as Ma Huang. While it may provide temporary relief from congestion, it can also increase heart rate and blood pressure, making a dry cough worse. Additionally, the herb Guaiacum officinale, also known as Guaiac or Guaiac wood, can be toxic in large quantities and may irritate the respiratory tract, making a dry cough more uncomfortable. Lastly, the herb Ipecacuanha (Cephalis ipecacuanha) contains a compound called ipecac, which can cause stomach upset and worsen a dry cough. It's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies to ensure your safety and well-being.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a thick, sweet liquid that can help soothe a dry cough. When we cough, our throat gets dry and irritated, but honey coats the throat and helps calm the irritation. It also has antibacterial properties that help fight off infections that can cause a cough. Some people take honey by the spoonful, while others mix it with warm water or tea to make a soothing drink.  Slippery elm is a type of tree bark that can also help with dry cough. The bark contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm the throat and stomach lining. When we swallow, the mucilage coats the throat and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can help relieve a dry cough and make it easier to swallow.  Gargling with salt water is another way to help with dry cough. Salt helps reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can cause a cough. When we gargle with salt water, the salt helps loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.  A warm bath or shower can also help with dry cough. The heat from the water helps loosen mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Some people also add eucalyptus oil or menthol to their bathwater, which can help open up the airways and relieve congestion.  Drinking warm liquids, such as tea or broth, can also help with dry cough. The heat from the liquid helps loosen mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Some people also add honey or lemon to their warm liquids, which can help soothe and calm the throat and stomach lining.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have a dry cough may also experience other ailments that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common issue is sinus congestion, which can be relieved with eucalyptus oil. This oil helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the sinuses. Another common issue is sore throats, which can be soothed with slippery elm bark. This herb forms a protective coating over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort. People with dry coughs may also experience bronchitis or asthma, which can be managed with thyme. This herb has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the airways and promote healing. Additionally, dry coughs can be caused by allergies, which can be treated with nettle leaf. This herb has natural anti-histamine properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Some people may also experience constipation due to dehydration, which can be treated with senna leaf. This herb helps to stimulate bowel movements and promote hydration.
